description: Per-item scoring weights by dimension and semantic differential integration formula. Items within a subscale carry equal weight (1.0) unless otherwise noted. Only self-report scorable items are included; non-scorable types (open_ended, writing_sample, process_narration) are handled by the NLP pipeline.

integration_formula.description: Final dimension score combines module item scores and semantic differential scores using a weighted blend

tier_1_high.description: High self-reportability -- respondents can accurately self-assess these dimensions. Self-report items are weighted at full value.

tier_2_moderate.description: Moderate self-reportability -- respondents have partial insight. Projective and behavioral items should be weighted more heavily when available.

contextual_flexibility.note: NLP-only dimension. M11-Q06 is a projective question targeting voice_aspiration, not contextual_flexibility. No self-report items contribute.

consistency.note: NLP-only dimension. No self-report items. Scored via cross-sample variance analysis.

notes.equal_weighting: All items within a dimension carry equal weight (1.0). This reflects the psychometric design where each item was calibrated to contribute equally to its target dimension.
notes.sd_normalization: Semantic differential scores are on a 1-7 scale. Normalize to 0-100 before applying the integration formula: normalized = ((raw - 1) / 6) * 100
notes.module_items_mean: Compute mean across all answered module items for the dimension. Missing items are excluded (not zero-filled) provided min_items_for_score is met.
notes.tier_2_adjustment: For Tier 2 (moderate self-reportability) dimensions, consider applying projective item preference when self-report and projective scores diverge significantly (see deep-dive-triggers.json self_perception_divergence).
notes.non_scorable_exclusion: Items of type open_ended, writing_sample, and process_narration are excluded from self-report scoring. They are scored computationally by the NLP pipeline (mivoca-nlp analyze-session) and contribute to observed scores in the calibration step.
